name: Herb-Crusted Cod with Roasted Tomato & Garlic Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 pound plum tomatoes cut into 1/4-inch slices
salt and freshly ground black pepper
four 7 to 8 ounce cod fillets -- pin bones removed
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450 degrees F.
2. Brush a baking sheet with olive oil and arrange the tomato slices and 3 garlic cloves in one layer. Brush tomatoes and garlic with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
3. Roast the tomatoes and garlic until lightly browned and the garlic is tender, about 20 minutes. Transfer to a blender. Add balsamic vinegar and 1 tablespoon water and puree until smooth. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper if necessary. Reserve sauce and keep warm.
4. In a food processor or blender, coarsely chop the remaining garlic clove. Add cilantro, chives, and parsley, processing until finely chopped. Transfer herb mixture to a shallow dish. Stir in bread crumbs, season with salt and pepper.
5. Brush cod fillets with olive oil and dredge in the herb and bread crumb mixture, ensuring even coating on all sides. Arrange coated fillets on a lightly oiled baking pan. Roast until just cooked through, 7 to 10 minutes.
6. Serve roasted cod over the sauce.
Yield: 4 servings
